features.summary: American Tower reported strong Q4 2024 results with property revenue growth of nearly 1% (3% FX-neutral) and adjusted EBITDA growth of approximately 2% (4% FX-neutral). The company achieved significant cost reductions with cash SG&A declining $35 million year-over-year and cash margin expansion of 140 basis points to 66.8%. For 2025, AMT expects organic tenant billings growth of approximately 5%, property revenue growth over 0.5%, and attributable AFFO per share of $10.40 representing 4% growth. The company successfully strengthened its balance sheet, reaching its 5x leverage target ahead of schedule and reducing floating rate debt exposure to 3%.
features.management_outlook: Management maintains a positive long-term outlook driven by sustained 5G investment cycles and emerging AI-driven demand. They expect mobile network capacity requirements to more than double over the next five years, with roughly half needing to be addressed through network densification. The company anticipates continued mid-single digit organic growth in developed markets through 2027, supported by ongoing 5G mid-band deployments which currently cover 65% of their U.S. portfolio sites. CoreSite data center business is expected to maintain strong double-digit growth with mid-teens stabilized yields on new development investments.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing carrier consolidation pressures, particularly in Latin America where elevated churn of approximately 5% is expected through 2027. Foreign exchange volatility continues to impact results with over 2% negative headwind from FX and straight-line revenue reductions. Interest rate uncertainty poses refinancing challenges with $3.7 billion in debt maturities during 2025, though the company has reduced floating rate exposure to mitigate this risk. Macroeconomic volatility across global markets and potential delays in customer equipment deployment timing could affect growth trajectories.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include accelerating 5G deployment activity with four consecutive quarters of sequential application growth in the U.S. and carriers upgrading from 50% to 65% mid-band coverage during 2024. Data center demand remains robust with record leasing activity and $80+ million backlog supporting continued expansion. The company is strategically reallocating capital toward higher-return developed markets, with 75% of unlevered AFFO expected from U.S., Canada, Europe and CoreSite operations. Operational efficiency initiatives and global cost management are driving margin expansion and supporting the path to sustained mid-to-high single-digit AFFO per share growth.
